name: Up-Next Intent
summary: A what-comes-next preview over an ordered sequence — surface the upcoming item (or items) so the viewer can anticipate it or skip ahead. The cross-media affordance shared by a slide deck's next-slide peek, a video player's up-next overlay, and a carousel's adjacent-item peek; a member of the advanceable-sequence family (#1179), not deck-specific. Owns only the preview surface and the skip-ahead action — the underlying ordering and advance mechanics belong to advanceable-sequence. Distinct from the overview-grid (the whole deck at once); up-next is forward-looking and bounded to the near future of the sequence.

depth.description: How much of the future is shown. next = just the immediately-upcoming item (the presenter next-slide peek); queue = an ordered list of upcoming items (a video playlist / a kiosk schedule).

surface.description: When the preview appears. persistent = always visible (presenter view's next-slide pane); on-approach = surfaces as the current item nears its end (the video up-next card before autoplay continues), composing timed-advance.

action.description: What the viewer can do with it. preview = look-ahead only; skip = act on it to jump ahead to the previewed item (skip the remainder of the current one).

description:
What's coming next

Across media, viewers want to see what comes next: a presenter glances at the next-slide pane, a video viewer sees the up-next card, a carousel peeks the adjacent item. The Up-Next intent names that shared affordance — a forward-looking preview of the near future of an ordered sequence.

Cross-media, not deck-specific

A member of the advanceable-sequence family (#1179): the same contract serves deck, video playlist, and carousel. It owns only the preview surface and the skip action; ordering and advance belong to the family kernel, and the appears-near-the-end behavior composes timed-advance.

Not the overview

Distinct from the overview-grid (a spatial view of the whole deck) — up-next is bounded to what follows now, and is the natural complement to it.
